what_it_does: This file is a living build-loop specification and chronological status log for the operator surface and right-hand-man guide. It records the current state, mission, stable mode and laws, the acceptance bar, sprint rules, phased plan, open questions, and a detailed status history with commits, verifications, and handoffs. It is meant to be the document the cron prompt points to while the cron itself only carries stable laws.
observations: Originated by Tim Geldard on commission 2026-06-17; all derived work attributed to him.
Explicitly split from the cron prompt: cron carries only stable mode+laws; this doc evolves in place to avoid stale-prompt drift.
Contains a CURRENT STATE snapshot at the top and a full STATUS LOG below.
Organized into Phase 0 (operator journey), Phase 1 (research), Phase 2 (V overlay shell), Phase 3 (gated tutorial/legibility), Phase 4 (decision surface).
Lists four open questions OQ1–OQ4 that gate freezing the legibility field-set.
Uses many channel IDs (ch-...), thread IDs (t-..., g-...), and commit hashes.
Heavy emphasis on verify-by-use at 390×844 first, then 1440×900, plus a fresh-eyes non-technical critic.
Operator-law: the operator never sees code, files, or machine names; meaning lives in registry data, not the instrument.
DNA owns the renderer; projection verifies elegant language by sight rather than building the render.
The spotlight mechanism is marked done: surface catalog, emit half, cadence, highlight punch, cold-stranger pass.
Many items remain: sector spotlight suppression, dynamic labels, dna/voice card copy, decided-card refresh, real take pending.
File is truncated at the end.
standouts: Cold-stranger legibility is the passed floor; the new bar is DNA-language fidelity (grammar/layout, voice, tokens).
The spotlight is end-to-end verified: brain emits point(token), surface highlights the live element, timing matches the V's words.
The decision surface has a host-integration contract and a prove-decision harness.
Registry-is-truth: meaning lives in registry meta/kinds/nodes, not hardcoded in the instrument.
Verify-by-use and fresh-eyes critic are treated as engineering gates, not optional QA.
conventions: Markdown with hierarchical headings and bullet lists.
Status entries use emoji markers: ✅ done, 🟡 residual, 🔴 blocked.
References people/streams by channel handle: composition, DNA, fork, recollection, lead.
Commit hashes and thread IDs are recorded inline.
Stable laws are labeled as mirrored in the cron.
No code symbols; the file is prose/spec only.
concerns: Extremely long and dense; the current-state snapshot and the status log overlap, so drift is possible.
Heavy reliance on external context (channels, commits, other files) makes it hard to read standalone.
Many remaining items and open questions suggest the spec is mid-flight.
The file is truncated, so the latest status is incomplete.
Some language is jargon-heavy despite the operator-law principle.
notes: This is the evolving source-of-truth build log for the operator surface + RHM guide, kept separate from the cron prompt and packed with verify-by-use checkpoints and handoffs.
questions: What exactly are the roles of DNA, fork, composition, recollection, and lead?
What does the truncated remainder of the file contain?
How does the cron prompt mechanically reference this file?
What is the current status of the real take/pending decisions?
What is 'Tim-on-phone' and what is required to close it?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the living spec the cron prompt points to, with stable laws elsewhere; the actual content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Registry-is-truth / instrument stays empty of meaning
Verify by real use, not code inspection
Replaceable components and clean swap seams
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent stale-prompt drift by separating stable laws from evolving plans, coordinate a multi-agent build across specialized streams, and enforce an honest, verification-first culture where nothing is declared done until it passes real use and fresh-eyes review.
novelty: The cron-prompt vs living-spec split, the operator-surface + RHM as a guided instrument, the prove-on-one decision harness, the live pointable spotlight catalog, and the cold-stranger pass as a build gate.
contribution: A structured, time-stamped build log that records what was built, how it was verified, what is blocked, and what was handed off; the operator-journey spine and legibility facets; the verification-first bar and anti-green-paint rules; and references to the host-integration contract and prove-decision harness.
summary_for_embedding: A living build-loop specification and chronological status log for an operator-facing surface and right-hand-man guide, recording current state, design laws, phases, open questions, and verified-by-use progress.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the evolving source of truth that a cron prompt references, preventing stale-prompt drift while coordinating a multi-stream build toward cold-stranger legibility.
